_meta.note: Verbatim content migrated from https://www.accesstrailsnova.com/ (Andrew O'Dell, SPARC Program Staff, Summer 2025 fieldwork). 'body' is an array of Andrew's verbatim lines/bullets; only zero-width spaces (U+200B) and Weebly leading '- ' bullet markers were stripped, and a stray space-before-period normalized. No wording changed. Category order is fixed: parking, trails, bathrooms, visitors_center, picnic_seating, signage. score:null renders as N/A (not penalized per the methodology). Scores out of 31 total across the five required categories (parking 6 + trails 7 + bathrooms 7 + picnic 5 + signage 6); visitors_center (max 5) is a non-penalized bonus.
_meta.image_source: archive/accesstrailsnova-weebly/images-orig/<name>_orig.<ext>; Phase 2 copies to accesstrails/img/parks/<slug>/<name>.<ext> and generates responsive derivatives

categories.label: Parking
body: Compliant number of ADA parking spaces
Limited number of aisles with sufficient width

categories.label: Trails
body: Trails are accessible but not marked
There are no resting areas, no shade, and no accessible seating

categories.label: Bathrooms
body: Bathrooms contain all the adequate features for ADA accessibility

categories.label: Visitors Center/Clubhouse
body: There is no visitors center at this park.

categories.label: Picnic Shelter & Seating Areas
body: None of the picnic areas, grills, or trash cans are accessible. They are all on the grass.

categories.label: Signage
body: Only the sign for the bathrooms contains the international symbol for accessibility.
Maps are at an accessible height, but do not indicate any accessible areas.

unique_feature.title: Nature Boardwalk
unique_feature.body: Huntley Meadows has a long nature boardwalk that is flat and maneuverable for wheelchairs. The boardwalk features beautiful nature and various wildlife. The best time to visit to see the wildlife is early in the morning.

categories.label: Parking
body: There are 3 designated accessible parking spaces

categories.label: Trails
body: There are two accessible trails, but they are not indicated on the signage.
One of the accessible trails begins at the edge of the parking lot.
The main boardwalk for the meadow is 1,904 feet from the parking lot.

categories.label: Bathrooms
body: There is a fully accessible bathroom available in the visitor's center during park hours from Monday-Friday, 9:00 AM to 5:00 PM on weekdays, and 9:00 AM to 1:00 PM on weekends.
There is an accessible porta potty in the parking lot, it is fairly dirty.

categories.label: Visitors Center/Clubhouse
body: There is a nice, staffed visitor's center open during park hours.
The visitor's center does not rent out their air conditioned rooms for events.
The visitor's center offers manual wheelchairs if needed.

categories.label: Picnic Shelter & Seating Areas
body: There is one picnic shelter, but it does not have tables or chairs, and is not for public use. It does have a roof/shelter.

categories.label: Signage
body: There are directions and information on signs at an accessible height, however they do not list accessible areas.
There is no Braille on the signs.

categories.label: Parking
body: There are multiple accessible parking spaces

categories.label: Trails
body: This park does not have any trails, it has a swimming pool and tennis courts.

categories.label: Bathrooms
body: There is a fully accessible bathroom available in the pool area, which has limited hours and is not open during the winter.

categories.label: Visitors Center/Clubhouse
body: There is no visitor's center. There is a pool house but it is only available to employees.

categories.label: Picnic Shelter & Seating Areas
body: Nice picnic area with one wheelchair accessible table.
There is an accessible grill.
The trash can is not accessible, however there is an accessible recycling bin near the tennis courts close by.

categories.label: Signage
body: There are not very many signs, but they do not contain Braille and may be somewhat far away from an accessible path.

unique_feature.title: Accessible Baseball Field
unique_feature.body: This park has an accessible baseball field and viewing area.

categories.label: Parking
body: There is plenty of accessible parking near the main shelter.

categories.label: Trails
body: There are long, fairly flat walking and biking trails.
The trail from the parking lot to the East trail system has one narrow area that is only 4 feet wide.
The path from the parking lot to the West trail system is wide and easily accessible.

categories.label: Bathrooms
body: There are no accessible bathrooms.
The bathrooms are marked with the international sign of accessibility, however there is a step to enter the bathroom and no maneuverability space, and no accessible features in the bathroom.

categories.label: Visitors Center/Clubhouse
body: There is no visitors center at this park.
There is a park service building at the main shelter with a small office only accessible to park staff.

categories.label: Picnic Shelter & Seating Areas
body: There are two picnic shelters, which are both accessible.
The main shelter is nice and spacious, and the path to the shelter is wide enough for a wheelchair, however watch for some overgrown bushes over the path.
The second shelter is down the West path, and has an accessible table.

categories.label: Signage
body: The whole area has both new and old signs, the newer signs have high contrast color and text, but the old signs don't.
None of the signs contain Braille, and some are higher than wheelchair height and could be hard to see.

unique_feature.title: Beautiful walking trails
unique_feature.body: Glencarlyn has a long trail system through the woods that runs along an idyllic stream.

categories.label: Parking
body: There is one fully accessible parking space near the main picnic area.
Note that there are no designated accessible parking spaces in the overflow parking lots.

categories.label: Trails
body: Trails are paved and designated accessible.
The beginning of the trailhead starts at the edge of the parking lot.
The trails are nice with a lot of shade and beautiful scenery.

categories.label: Bathrooms
body: There are accessible bathrooms with adequate maneuvering space.
The bathrooms are air conditioned.

categories.label: Visitors Center/Clubhouse
body: There is no visitors center at this park.

categories.label: Picnic Shelter & Seating Areas
body: All of the shelters are accessible.
All of the trash cans are accessible.

categories.label: Signage
body: There are no visible directional signs or maps of the park.
There are no signs containing Braille.
The signs that are at the park are at a readable height for wheelchairs with high contrast text.

unique_feature.title: Accessible Playground
unique_feature.body: This park has playground features accessible for wheelchairs.

categories.label: Parking
body: There is one fully accessible paved parking space in a gravel parking lot.

categories.label: Trails
body: There is a very short section of flat, gravel trail that runs alongside the park, but as soon as it gets to the woods it becomes rough and inaccessible to wheelchairs.

categories.label: Bathrooms
body: There is a clean, large, accessible porta potty on site near the shelter.

categories.label: Visitors Center/Clubhouse
body: There is no visitors center at this park.

categories.label: Picnic Shelter & Seating Areas
body: There is a fully accessible shelter beside the parking lot with an accessible table in a well placed location.
There is an accessible grill at the picnic structure.
There are accessible trash cans and pet cleanup bags.

categories.label: Signage
body: There is a good, wheelchair height, sign at the start of the paved section of the trail.
The sign includes slope and cross slope percentages.

categories.label: Parking
body: The accessible parking spaces are marked with the international accessibility symbol.
There is adequate maneuverability around the accessible parking.

categories.label: Trails
body: The trail gets slightly steep from the playground down to the main trail, but is manageable.
After about a quarter mile it turns into a bike trail with two lanes so bikes can maneuver around a wheelchair.
There are no signs indicating how to get to the main trailhead.

categories.label: Bathrooms
body: There is an accessible bathroom at the main picnic structure.
There is also an accessible porta potty in the parking lot.

categories.label: Visitors Center/Clubhouse
body: There is no visitors center at this park.

categories.label: Picnic Shelter & Seating Areas
body: There is an accessible table in the shelter, however it was up against a pillar which would make it difficult to maneuver to.
The grills and trash cans are accessible.

categories.label: Signage
body: There are not many signs, and there are no signs down to the trailhead which is not immediately visible from the parking lot.

unique_feature.title: Accessible Viewing Area of the Falls
unique_feature.body: This park has a specifically designated accessible viewing area of the falls at Overlook 3.

categories.label: Parking
body: There is plenty of accessible parking with adequate maneuvering space near the trail entrance that leads to the visitor's center.

categories.label: Trails
body: The trail is small gravel and mostly flat, but has some bumps that have to be maneuvered around.
The path leading to the overlook area has an accessible ramp.
Note that only Overlook 3 has an accessible viewing area.
The falls are partially visible at wheelchair height at Overlook 2.

categories.label: Bathrooms
body: There is a fully accessible bathroom down the ramp from the visitor's center and near the snack bar.

categories.label: Visitors Center/Clubhouse
body: The visitor's center is very nice with guides and maneuverable space.
Note that the electronic door was broken as of mid-August 2025, but there are guides staffing the center during park hours who could assist with the doors.

categories.label: Picnic Shelter & Seating Areas
body: There are accessible tables, however they are near the bathrooms which, during the hotter days in summer, did not smell pleasant.
All of the other picnic tables are on the grass and may be difficult to traverse in a wheelchair.

categories.label: Signage
body: There is plenty of clear, directional and designated signage throughout the park.

categories.label: Parking
body: There is plenty of accessible parking near the trailhead, but you have to drive past the event parking spaces and through the employee parking to get there.
The accessible parking near the trailhead is not available on days when there are concerts unless you arrive in the morning.

categories.label: Trails
body: The trail on the left toward the gazebo is partially paved and mostly flat.
The trail to the right is rougher and would be more difficult to maneuver in a wheelchair.

categories.label: Bathrooms
body: There is a family accessible bathroom with hand rails and maneuverable space.
The soap and hand dryer are out of reach for wheelchair height.

categories.label: Visitors Center/Clubhouse
body: The concert venue is accessible from venue parking with a clear path to accessible seating areas.

categories.label: Picnic Shelter & Seating Areas
body: There are multiple, well placed accessible tables in the seating area.
The seating area is uncovered.
The amphitheater in the woods at the end of the trail also has accessible seating areas.
The trash cans are out of reach.

categories.label: Signage
body: The signage is near the main amphitheater and there are no directional signs that guide you to it.
There is no Braille.
